channel.title: Fine-Tuning on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Fine-Tuning on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Self-serve custom photo avatar creation in Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry has generally available self-serve custom photo avatar creation in the Foundry NextGen fine-tuning portal. It is aimed at enterprise customers building voice agents or avatar-and-speech content who need a branded avatar.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Benchmark evaluations for fine-tuned models in Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry now offers public preview benchmark evaluations for fine-tuned models. The feature is aimed at comparing model performance on tasks such as reasoning, math, and domain knowledge, where leaderboard results may not reflect real-world behavior.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: ai: What’s new in Microsoft Foundry | May 2026
item.description:
May 2026 Microsoft Foundry updates include trace-based evaluation for any agent on any cloud, new catalog models (Grok 4.3 and DeepSeek V4), GPT-5 Reinforcement Fine-Tuning at gated GA, and several platform and SDK additions. The post also notes GA for Managed VNET and Content Understanding improvements, plus Foundry Local updates and a new azure-ai-projects release.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: ai: What’s New in Microsoft Foundry Fine-Tuning | April 2026
item.description:
April 2026 adds three Reinforcement Fine-Tuning updates in Microsoft Foundry: global training for o4-mini with lower per-token pricing in 12+ regions, new GPT-4.1-based model graders for improved reward signals, and a best-practices guide for RFT workflows.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog
